summary:
Iteration 169 frontend-only testing of NEW adviser client profile tabs (Retirement + Profile & Inputs) on /client-360, AdviserClientInputs manual data entry, P3 refactors (App.js extraction + event-based PortfolioProvider). 13/13 feature groups PASS. Core flows verified: (1) Both new tabs 'Retirement' and 'Profile & Inputs' render on Client360View with correct data-testids (tab-content-retirement, tab-content-profile-inputs). (2) Retirement tab embeds full RetirementWorkshop (data-testid=retirement-workshop-page) with 2 default scenarios (Current Plan 98% / $11.88M / $4.34M AUD and Aggressive Savings 100% / $13.80M / $6.74M) — all 6 required metric testids present (scenario-metrics-{id}, metric-confidence-{id}, metric-at-retirement-{id}, metric-p10-{id}) and all 8 scenario tabs present (tab-budget/investments/goals/assumptions × 2). (3) Profile & Inputs tab renders AdviserClientInputs (data-testid=adviser-client-inputs) with ALL 7 sub-tabs (Personal/Income/Expenses/Assets/Liabilities/Goals/Protection) + Save + Reset buttons. (4) 4 rollup cards display formatted AUD: Annual Income $185,000, Annual Expenses $108,000, Net Worth $0 (test client had empty asset array), Annual Surplus $77,000. (5) Personal tab client name input updates header: typing 'Test Family 42' updated H2 to 'Adviser Data Entry · Test Family 42'. (6) All 4 Add Row buttons work (ip-add-income/asset/liability/goal). (7) Save button persists to localStorage key adviser_inputs_client_1 and shows 'Client inputs saved' toast. (8) Reset clears localStorage. (9) /retirement-confidence standalone still renders RetirementWorkshop with Layout wrapper. (10) /dashboard still loads and shows Thompson client data (portfolioSeedData extraction intact — Total Wealth $1.61M rendered correctly). Fixed minor DOM-nesting warning in AdviserClientInputs.jsx line 337 ( rendered
inside

, changed outer

to

).

issues: Recharts ResponsiveContainer logs 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' warnings on first render inside embedded tab — pre-existing (flagged iteration 168). Cosmetic — chart still renders.

action_items:
(RESOLVED by testing agent) AdviserClientInputs.jsx line 337: outer wrapper was

containing a which renders as

— React logged HTML hydration error 'In HTML,
cannot be a descendant of

'. Changed

to

(kept identical className). Verify diff & confirm intent.
critical_code_review_comments: AdviserClientInputs.jsx (342 lines) is a single-file form with 7 sub-tabs — approaching the 700-line split threshold. If additional sections (e.g., tax, super, business interests) are added, consider splitting each tab into its own component file under /components/adviser-inputs/ for maintainability.

AdviserClientInputs.jsx useEffect deps are [clientId, client]: since `client` is an object re-created on each render in Client360View (passed as plain object), this useEffect will fire on every parent re-render and reset unsaved edits. Consider memoising the client prop in Client360View, or deps=[clientId] with a ref check, to avoid losing unsaved input on parent re-render.
AdviserClientInputs.jsx DEFAULT_DATA goals hardcode targetYear as currentYear+17/+10/+7 instead of deriving from client's retirement age — minor, not regression.

Client360View Retirement tab now triggers 500 Monte Carlo sims × 2 scenarios on every tab mount/expand — iteration 168 comment about debouncing still applies when users edit Budget/Invest/Goals/Assum. inputs.
RetirementWorkshop.jsx `embedded` prop wrap is clean but the embedded case still runs internal Layout.jsx side-effects? Confirmed it does NOT — `embedded=true` skips <Layout> wrap. Good.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 13/13 feature groups pass. (1) 2 new tabs visible (2) Retirement tab embeds RW with all required testids (3) Profile & Inputs tab renders AdviserClientInputs + 7 sub-tabs + Save/Reset (4) rollup cards render $ amounts (5) Personal tab name→header sync works (6) Add-row buttons for income/asset/liability/goal all fire (7) Expense edits persist via state (8) Save→localStorage + toast 'saved' (9) Reset→clears localStorage (10) /retirement-confidence standalone unchanged (11) /dashboard Thompson data unchanged (portfolioSeedData extraction intact) (12) No console errors apart from pre-existing Recharts width(-1) warning (13) Hydration warning fixed.

test_credentials: N/A — localStorage only. Set wealth_command_compliance_v5='permanent', app_mode='adviser', selected_client=JSON client_1 (Thompson Family) before /client-360 navigation.
seed_data_creation: Test client seeded via localStorage: id=client_1, profile.name='David & Sarah Thompson', incomeHousehold=185000, age=50, children=2, riskProfile=Balanced. No backend seeding needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All NEW features (Retirement + Profile & Inputs tabs on Client360View, AdviserClientInputs component with 7 sub-tabs, Save/Reset localStorage persistence, 4 rollup cards) work end-to-end. The data-testids requested in the review are all present and verified. The P3 refactors (App.js → portfolioSeedData.js, PortfolioProvider event-based instead of 500ms polling) did NOT break /dashboard or /retirement-confidence. One minor code-quality issue flagged: AdviserClientInputs useEffect re-runs on every parent re-render because `client` prop is a new object reference each render — could reset unsaved edits if Client360View re-renders. Main agent should review and consider memoising the client prop or changing the dep array. Net Worth displays $0 in rollup when client.assets is empty (test condition) — this is expected. Event-based client-changed CustomEvent wasn't stress-tested because navigation test wasn't the focus of this iteration's request, but the PortfolioProvider didn't log errors and /dashboard reflected correct Thompson data.
rca of the issue:
Minor DOM nesting bug fixed: AdviserClientInputs.jsx line 337 used

as outer wrapper for footer text containing a component. (shadcn) renders as

. React strict/hydration mode logs 'In HTML,
cannot be a descendant of

' and on SSR/hydration can re-mount children. Fix: changed outer

to

, kept same className for identical typography. No functional change to rendered layout or feature behaviour.
